bplusrecv.summary: Receives a file from the host using the B-Plus protocol. Blocks until the transfer completes; sets result to 1 on success, 0 on failure. Files are saved to the file transfer folder.

bplussend.summary: Sends <filename> to the host using the B-Plus protocol. Blocks until the transfer completes; sets result to 1 on success, 0 on failure. Relative paths resolve against the file transfer folder.

callmenu.summary: Executes the Tera Term menu item identified by <menu ID> (IDs are listed in the Keycode reference).

changedir.summary: Changes Tera Term's file transfer directory to <path>. Relative paths in file transfer commands resolve against it. Differs from setdir, which changes the macro's working directory.

clearscreen.summary: Clears the screen depending on <int>: 0 clears the VT window, 1 clears the scroll buffer and VT window, 2 clears the TEK window.

closett.summary: Closes Tera Term and puts the macro in an unlinked state, suppressing TCP/IP disconnect confirmation. A later connect command can open a new Tera Term and re-link the macro.

connect.summary: Links the macro to Tera Term and optionally connects to a host with the given command-line parameters. Sets result to 0 (no link), 1 (linked, not connected), or 2 (linked and connected); ignored if already linked and connected.

cygconnect.summary: Connects to Cygwin (CygTerm) via Tera Term, passing the given parameters to CygTerm. Sets result to 0 (no link), 1 (linked, not connected), or 2 (linked and connected); ignored if already linked and connected.

disconnect.summary: Closes the connection between Tera Term and the host. <confirm> 0 disconnects without a confirmation dialog; nonzero (default) shows the dialog. The macro-to-Tera Term link is kept.

dispstr.summary: Displays the given strings or characters on the Tera Term terminal screen; integer values are shown as the ASCII character of their lowest byte. Output is local only and may desync the buffer from the host. Escape sequences are supported.

enablekeyb.summary: Enables (<flag> 1) or disables (<flag> 0) user keyboard input during macro execution; when disabled the user cannot type while the macro sends data.

flushrecv.summary: Clears the MACRO receive buffer that holds characters received from the host, discarding stale data so it does not interfere with later character-reading commands such as wait.

gethostname.summary: Stores the destination hostname or IP address of Tera Term into the string variable <strvar>.

getmodemstatus.summary: Stores serial port modem control-register signal status into <intvar> as a bit value: CTS(1), DSR(2), ring indicator(4), RLSD(8). Sets result to 0 on success, 1 on failure.

gettitle.summary: Stores the Tera Term window title text into the string variable <strvar>.

getttpos.format: getttpos <showflag> <window x> <window y> <window width> <window height> <client x> <client y> <client width> <client height>
getttpos.summary: Retrieves the VT window's show state (normal/minimized/maximized/hidden) and the pixel position and size of the window and client areas into the given variables. Sets result to 0 on success, -1 on failure.

kmtfinish.summary: Tells Tera Term to perform the Kermit Finish operation. Blocks until it completes; sets result to 1 on success, 0 otherwise.

kmtget.summary: Retrieves <filename> from the host using the Kermit Get protocol (host must be in server mode). Blocks until done; sets result to 1 on success, 0 on failure. Relative paths save to the file transfer folder.

kmtrecv.summary: Receives a file using the Kermit protocol. Blocks until the transfer completes; sets result to 1 on success, 0 on failure. Files are saved to the file transfer folder.

kmtsend.summary: Sends <filename> to the host using the Kermit protocol. Blocks until the transfer completes; sets result to 1 on success, 0 on failure. Relative paths resolve against the file transfer folder.

loadkeymap.summary: Tells Tera Term to load the keyboard setup (keymap) file specified by <filename>.

logautoclosemode.summary: Controls whether an open log file is closed automatically when the macro ends: <flag> 1 enables, 0 disables. The setting reverts to its default when the macro terminates.

logclose.summary: Closes the currently open log file, ending the logging started by logopen.

loginfo.summary: Stores the current log filename into <strvar> and sets result to a bitwise flag combination of the log settings (binary 1, append 2, plain text 4, timestamp 8, hide dialog 16), or -1 if logging is not active.

logopen.format: logopen <filename> <binary flag> <append flag> [<plain text flag> [<timestamp flag> [<hide dialog flag> [<include screen buffer flag> [<timestamp type>]]]]]
logopen.summary: Starts logging received characters to <filename>. Binary flag writes data unmodified; append flag appends vs. overwrites; optional flags control plain text, timestamps, dialog hiding, screen buffer, and timestamp type. Sets result to 0 on success, 1 on failure.

logpause.summary: Temporarily suspends logging; characters received while paused are discarded. Logging can be resumed with logstart.

logrotate.summary: Configures automatic log file rotation by file size, sets how many rotated copies to keep, or halts rotation. A log file must already be open; rotation occurs automatically, not immediately.

logstart.summary: Resumes logging that was suspended by logpause, continuing to record to the log file.

logwrite.summary: Appends <string> to the log file. Works only while a log is open (including while paused); an empty string causes a syntax error.

quickvanrecv.summary: Receives a file from the host using the Quick-VAN protocol, pausing until the transfer completes; sets system variable result to 1 on success, 0 otherwise. Files are saved to the file transfer folder.

quickvansend.summary: Sends a file to the host using the Quick-VAN protocol, pausing until the transfer completes; sets result to 1 on success, 0 otherwise. Relative paths resolve to the file transfer folder.

recvln.summary: Receives one line of characters and stores it in the system variable inputstr, blocking until a line is received, the connection ends, or a timeout occurs. Sets result to 1 on success, 0 otherwise; typically used in a loop after setsync 1.

recvfile.summary: Writes data received from the host to a file unchanged (binary mode fixed, no conversions). If no data arrives within the wait time (seconds), reception stops and result is set to 1, otherwise 0; relative paths use the file transfer folder.

restoresetup.summary: Causes Tera Term to load the Tera Term setup file specified by <filename>.

scprecv.summary: Receives a remote file from the host using the SCP protocol. If no local filename is given, the file is copied to the local current directory. Runs asynchronously (does not wait). Available in version 4.57 or later.

scpsend.summary: Sends a local file to the remote host using the SCP protocol. If no destination is given, the file is copied to the remote home directory. Runs asynchronously (does not wait). Available in version 4.57 or later.

send.summary: Sends characters to the host. String data is transmitted as text; an integer's lowest byte (0-255) is sent as an ASCII character. Text is sent like sendtext, binary like sendbinary.

sendbinary.summary: Sends characters to the host without modification (strings sent as UTF-8, an integer's lowest byte sent as an ASCII character). Data is forwarded unchanged with no character-code conversion; use sendtext to convert codes.

sendbreak.summary: Sends a break signal to the host. Raises a break on serial consoles, sends IAC BREAK (255 243) on TELNET, and SSH2_MSG_CHANNEL_REQUEST(break) on SSH2.

sendbroadcast.summary: Broadcasts characters to all terminals and then sends them to the host (strings as-is, integer's lowest byte as ASCII). Data is ignored on terminals where AcceptBroadcast is disabled. Available in version 4.62 or later.

sendfile.summary: Sends a file to the host and waits for the transfer to complete. A non-zero binary flag sends the file unchanged; zero converts newlines (CR to CR/CRLF) and removes control characters except TAB, LF, CR. Relative paths use the file transfer folder.

sendkcode.summary: Performs the function defined for a key or key combination (key code per KEYCODE.EXE) the given number of times, typically sending a string to the host. Cannot run macros (control flag 2) or menu commands (control flag 3); use callmenu for menu commands.

sendln.summary: Sends characters followed by a newline to the host; data format is the same as send. May be called with no arguments to send only a newline.

sendlnbroadcast.summary: Broadcasts characters followed by a newline to all terminals and then sends them to the host; data format is the same as sendbroadcast. Data is ignored on terminals where AcceptBroadcast is disabled.

sendlnmulticast.summary: Multicasts characters followed by a newline to the terminals selected by <multicastname> and then sends them to the host; data format is the same as sendmulticast. Available in version 4.96 or later.

sendmulticast.summary: Sends data to the multicast terminals named by <multicastname> (set first with setmulticastname); strings sent as-is, an integer's lowest byte as ASCII. Data is ignored on terminals where AcceptBroadcast is disabled.

sendtext.summary: Sends characters to the host (strings as text, an integer's lowest byte as ASCII); the text is received and converted to character code before transmission. Use sendbinary to bypass conversion. Available in version 5.3 or later.

setbaud.summary: Changes the serial port speed. Version 4.66 and later accept the actual baud rate (e.g. 115200); earlier versions use a speed ID number (1-15). Available in version 4.58 or later.

setdebug.summary: Configures Tera Term's debug mode: 0 = off (normal output), 1 = usual debug mode, 2 = hex output (received bytes printed in space-separated uppercase hex), 3 = output disabled. Available in version 4.64 or later.

setdtr.summary: Sets the DTR (Data Terminal Ready) line: non-zero enables, zero disables. Only works on serial connections with flow control set to none. Available in version 4.59 or later.

setecho.summary: Sets local echo: a non-zero echo flag enables local echo, zero disables it.

setflowctrl.summary: Changes serial flow control: 1 = Xon/Xoff, 2 = hardware RTS/CTS, 3 = none, 4 = hardware DSR/DTR (4.105+). Available in version 4.94 or later.

setmulticastname.summary: Sets the terminal's own multicast identification name, used as the target for multicast send commands. Available in version 4.62 or later.

setrts.summary: Sets the RTS (Request To Send) line: non-zero enables, zero disables. Only works on serial connections with flow control set to none. Available in version 4.59 or later.

setserialdelaychar.summary: Changes the time interval (in milliseconds) between individual characters sent by Tera Term over serial. Available in version 5.5.0 or later.

setserialdelayline.summary: Changes the time interval (in milliseconds) between lines sent by Tera Term over serial. Available in version 5.5.0 or later.

setspeed.summary: Changes the serial port speed to the specified bps value; an alias for setbaud. Available in version 4.99 or later.

setsync.summary: Sets synchronous (non-zero) or asynchronous (zero, the default) communication mode. Synchronous mode prevents buffer overflow by pausing reception when the buffer is full, but slows reception; use it only when needed.

settitle.summary: Changes Tera Term's window title to the string given in <title>.

showtt.summary: Controls window visibility/state by flag: VT window (-1 hide, 0 minimize, 1 restore), TEK window (2 hide, 3 minimize, 4 open/restore, 5 close), Log window (6 hide, 7 minimize, 8 restore).

testlink.summary: Reports the current link and connection status in system variable result: 0 = no link to Tera Term, 1 = linked but not connected to host, 2 = both link and connection established.

unlink.summary: Terminates the link between the current Tera Term window and the macro, leaving the macro with no control over the window. The connect command can later link to a new window.

wait.summary: Pauses until one of up to 10 strings is received from the host, or until timeout. An empty string waits for one character. Sets result to which string matched (1-10), or 0 on timeout; timeout governed by timeout/mtimeout variables.

wait4all.summary: Pauses until one of up to 10 strings is received across all macro-linked terminals, or until timeout; only terminals linked when the command runs are monitored. Sets result to which string matched (1-10), or 0 on timeout. Requires Wait4allMacroCommand enabled in teraterm.ini.

waitevent.summary: Pauses until one of the specified events occurs: 1 (timeout), 2 (unlink), 4 (disconnection), 8 (connection), combinable. Sets system variable result to the identifier of the event that occurred.

waitln.summary: Pauses until a line containing one of up to 10 strings is received, or until timeout; an empty string waits for any line. Stores the received line in inputstr and sets result to which string matched (1-10), or 0 on timeout.

waitn.summary: Pauses until the given number of bytes is received from the host, or until timeout. Stores the received characters (max 511 bytes) in inputstr and sets result to 1 on success, 0 on timeout. Available in version 4.62 or later.

waitrecv.summary: Pauses until a received string of length <len> bytes contains <sub-string> at position <pos> (1-indexed), or until timeout; stores the received string in inputstr. Sets result to 1 on match, 0 on timeout, or -1 if the substring matched but the length was short due to timeout.

waitregex.format: waitregex <string1 with regular expression> [<string2 with regular expression> ...]
waitregex.summary: Pauses until a received line (max 511 chars) matches one of up to 10 Oniguruma regex patterns, or until timeout. Sets result to which pattern matched (1-10) or 0 on timeout, stores the line in inputstr, the matched text in matchstr, and capture groups in groupmatchstr1-9.

xmodemrecv.summary: Receives a file from the host using the XMODEM protocol, pausing until transfer completes; sets result to 1 on success, 0 on failure. Binary flag non-zero for binary files; option selects Checksum/CRC/1K (option 3 = CRC with 1K packets in 4.90+). Relative paths use the file transfer folder.

xmodemsend.summary: Sends a file using the XMODEM protocol, pausing until transfer completes; sets result to 1 on success, 0 on failure. Option 1 = Checksum, 2 = CRC, 3 = 1K packet size; relative paths use the file transfer folder.

ymodemrecv.summary: Receives a file from the host using the YMODEM protocol, pausing until transfer completes; sets result to 1 on success, 0 on failure. Relative paths use the file transfer folder. Available in version 4.66 or later.

ymodemsend.summary: Sends the file <filename> to the host using the YMODEM protocol, pausing until transfer completes; sets result to 1 on success, 0 on failure. Relative paths use the file transfer folder.

zmodemrecv.summary: Receives a file from the host using the ZMODEM protocol, pausing until transfer completes; sets result to 1 on success, 0 on failure. Relative paths use the file transfer folder.

zmodemsend.summary: Sends the file <filename> to the host using the ZMODEM protocol, pausing until transfer completes; sets result to 1 on success, 0 on failure. Binary flag non-zero for binary files; relative paths use the file transfer folder.
